rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the intended target component or implementation while preserving AADL architecture
Place component type annexes in valid component type scopes and implementation annexes in valid implementation scopes
Use only visible identifiers in contracts; avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Preserve existing AADL structure and connections when inserting AGREE annex blocks
generic_rules: Maintain consistent scoping rules between AADL and AGREE annex blocks
Ensure all identifiers used in AGREE contracts are visible in the target scope
Preserve the original AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Validate that all inserted AGREE annex blocks are syntactically correct and type-checked
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Place assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes
Use implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes
Use AGREE logical operators such as `and`, `or`, `not(...)`, and implication `=>`
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Avoid placing subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not use opaque Boolean data range comparisons unless the requirement and AADL data representation provide a numeric scalar
Ensure all identifiers used in AGREE contracts are visible in the target scope
